dashboard.label: T1567.002 — Cloud Storage Exfiltration
dashboard.description: Detects data staging to cloud-storage SaaS by combining IP-IOC enrichment (cloud_storage_ips.csv) with an uplink-bytes threshold. Modern ransomware staging pattern.

search.query: `cloud_exfil` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | stats sum(total_orig_bytes) AS bytes | eval gb = round(bytes / 1024 / 1024 / 1024, 2) | eval mb = round(bytes / 1024 / 1024, 0) | eval display = if(gb >= 1, gb . " GB", mb . " MB") | table display

`cloud_exfil` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | eval window=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M") | eval bytes_mb=round(total_orig_bytes/1024/1024, 1) | sort -total_orig_bytes | table window, src, cloud_service, bytes_mb, record_count, distinct_destinations, sample_destinations

panel.title: Uplink bytes by service, over time

search.query: index=zeek sourcetype=zeek:conn | lookup cloud_storage_ips.csv ip AS "id.resp_h" OUTPUT cloud_service | where isnotnull(cloud_service) | timechart span=1h sum(orig_bytes) BY cloud_service limit=8

html.h3: T1567.002 — Exfiltration to Cloud Storage Service
html.p: Modern ransomware operators stage stolen data on commodity cloud storage before encryption — Mega.nz, Dropbox, WeTransfer, AnonFiles, Gofile, Transfer.sh, pCloud, MediaFire, etc. — using tools like rclone or robocopy. The destination is allow-listed by every egress firewall on Earth; the only signal is the direction and volume of the bytes.

The detection is two-signal: (1) destination IP matches cloud_storage_ips.csv AND (2) cumulative orig_bytes per (src, service) over a 1-h window ≥ 50 MB. Per-record gate of 1 MB suppresses normal user reads. Allow-list known IT staff in cloud_user_allowlist.csv.
